user_content:
Write me the beginning of a script (formatted like a real movie script) for a comedy movie scene, based on the below information:

Andy, a man in his 40s, is the Head of Computing at a rural high school. He has a loud, deep voice, and has more confidence than he has intelligence. He wants to be friends with his colleague exam invigilator John, so often tries and fails to impress him with his elaborate stories. He often bangs on about how he presents the school talent show. He has poor personal hygiene and can often be found making moaning noises while using disabled toilets, despite him having no obvious disability. Andy wears an untidy, stained white shirt. When addressing a group he starts with the words "Right guys, settle down".

Roger, a man in his 50s, is a computing teacher working under Andy. He is an intelligent man, with grey hair and a laid back attitude to teaching. His classes are often out of control, while he sits there in despair. He has quiet contempt at the fact that Andy is the head of department, because he knows that he is much more qualified  …[truncated]
I really like the "Blazer and Blouse" suggestion. I have a cashmere blend cardigan that I've been wearing a lot lately, do you think it would work as a substitute for a blazer?
Can you describe the introduction of CGI in The Walking Dead, and how it affected the show's atmosphere?

The following is the continuation of the chapter. Summarize this as well:

virtual reality and 3D interaction
positioning in 3D space
moving and grasping
seeing 3D (helmets and caves)
positioning in 3D space
• cockpit and virtual controls
– steering wheels, knobs and dials … just like real!
• the 3D mouse
– six-degrees of movement: x, y, z + roll, pitch, yaw
• data glove
– fibre optics used to detect finger position
• VR helmets
– detect head motion and possibly eye gaze
• whole body tracking
– accelerometers strapped to limbs or reflective dots
and video processing
pitch, yaw and roll
pitch
yaw
roll
3D displays
• desktop VR
– ordinary screen, mouse or keyboard control
– perspective and motion give 3D effect
• seeing in 3D
– use stereoscopic vision
– VR helmets
– screen plus shuttered specs, etc.
also see extra slides on 3D vision
VR headsets
• small TV screen for each eye
• slightly different angles
• 3D effect
VR motion sickness
• time delay
– move head … lag … display moves
– conflict: head movement vs. eyes
• depth perception
– headset gives different stereo d …[truncated]

I think I'll try the outfit with the belt. I have a thin, patent leather belt in black that I think would work well with the boots and trousers. I'll wear the cardigan open to showcase my waist and add some texture with the belt. Thanks for the advice!

I'm looking for some outfit inspiration. I recently got a beautiful silk blouse from a local boutique in my neighborhood, and I want to know how to style it for a work event. Can you give me some suggestions?
I like that idea. I also have a pair of high-waisted trousers from Zara that I recently got. Do you think they would work well with the cardigan and blouse?
